campaign: Honest roofline correction to PR #1698 wedge probe over-claim
verdict_tier: RED — CLAIM RETIRED (W1 small-M GEMV wedge falsified by honest roofline)
previous_claim_retired: PR #1698 W1 small-M GEMV: '99.05% sub-roofline at M=1, ceiling 5-10x estimated'
correction_reason: the 99.05% number was computed against FP32 COMPUTE peak (31.2 TFLOPS), the wrong reference for memory-bound GEMV. AI = 0.50 FLOPS/byte at M=1 is far below the memory-bound threshold (~46 FLOPS/byte), so HBM bandwidth is the relevant roofline, not FP32 compute peak.
method: cudaMemcpy DtoD 256 MB timed (20w + 200m median) + cuBLAS SGEMM sweep

honest_ceiling_per_M.M_1: 0% (cuBLAS 102.71% of HBM roof = AT roofline within noise) → W1 wedge does NOT exist
honest_ceiling_per_M.M_128: transitional — AI 60 crosses compute-bound threshold; HBM% drops as compute% rises (intersection region, not a wedge)
honest_ceiling_per_M.M_1024: compute-bound region (24% sub-compute-peak) — this is BC2 standalone GEMM territory (already covered, [x] in round-7)

finding: cuBLAS SGEMM at small M (1-32) is ALREADY at HBM bandwidth roofline within measurement noise. W1 small-M GEMV wedge identified in PR #1698 is a phantom wedge — there is no meaningful speedup available because cuBLAS already saturates the binding constraint (HBM bandwidth for memory-bound regime).
axiom_correction: the 'sub-roofline' metric in PR #1698 wedge probe used FP32 compute peak as the universal reference, but the correct reference depends on the kernel's arithmetic intensity. Memory-bound kernels (AI < peak_compute / peak_BW) are bounded by HBM bandwidth, not compute peak.

implications_for_round_11_GPU_md.W1_status: RETIRE (phantom wedge, falsified by honest HBM roofline measurement)
implications_for_round_11_GPU_md.W2_status: needs re-examination — top-K ceiling was measured with thrust::sort O(N log N), not production cub::DeviceSegmentedRadixSort O(N). The 1.80x at M=8 LLaMA-vocab likely shrinks under proper top-K. Real ceiling lower-bound = HBM-bandwidth-bound radix sort: read+write M*N once = bounded by HBM. Crude estimate: cublas_ms (HBM-bound at small M) + N·M / HBM ≈ topk_ms_HBM_bound. If both at HBM roof, ceiling ~1.0x. Needs measurement with cub.
implications_for_round_11_GPU_md.W3_status: grouped QKV stride debug — even if fixed, the wedge is bounded by HBM at small M (input X share saves at most M·K·4 bytes per replicated read = 1 of 3 reads = 33% HBM saved → 1.5x ceiling AT MOST, but each separate SGEMM is HBM-bound and BW-shared via 1-stream serialization, so practical ceiling < 1.5x).

remaining_real_wedges: BC4 attention with proper occupancy (open at 5.3-6.9x slower — real >>1x wedge if occupancy fixed)
Light-inner-kernel fusions where the inner work is NOT GEMM
retired_wedges: W1 small-M GEMV (this verdict — cuBLAS already at HBM roof for M ≤ 32)
needs_remeasure: W2 top-K fused (with proper cub::DeviceSegmentedRadixSort baseline)

methodology_lesson: instrument-first methodology (cheap-first oracle) requires the CORRECT roofline. Using FP32 compute peak as a universal reference is a common mistake that surfaces phantom wedges for memory-bound kernels. The honest roofline per arithmetic intensity (min(compute_peak, HBM_BW × AI)) is the right reference. ADD to feedback_instrument_first_methodology: rule 5 — 'roofline reference must match the kernel's binding constraint (compute vs memory) per AI'.
